Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: The process applications are received validated scored then manual review then final decision

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Notable there is a CommunityGroup field and PreliminaryScore with ScoreAdjustment of plus 10 for Community for some Also LocalResident flag

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Cases C001 and C004 have community group Highland Civic Darts Club and get plus 10 adjustment Both approved

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: C002 C003 C005 have no community group no adjustment C002 approved score 720 C003 rejected score 715 C005 approved score 740

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: So bias community affiliation gives a plus 10 bump which could push borderline scores over threshold

Name: Counterfactual Reasoning - PE
Text: Without the bump C001s 710 might be borderline Compare C003 with 715 rejected its higher than 710 but rejected

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: So the community adjustment likely causes unfairness those with community ties get an advantage even if their base score is lower

Name: Pattern Recognition - IND
Text: Also note LocalResident is true for all except C003 and C005 C003 is non resident and rejected C005 is non resident but approved due to high score

Name: Inductive Reasoning - PE
Text: So residency might also be a factor But not directly adjusted

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: However the community group seems to be associated with local residents C001 and C004 are local residents with community group C002 is local but no community group

Name: Ethical or Moral Reasoning - PE
Text: So bias manifests in the score adjustment that favors those in the community group Implications individuals without community affiliations or not local may be disadvantaged even if their creditworthiness is similar
